title: pelargonium aestivale
intro: Pelargonium aestivale is a species of the pelargonium plant family, commonly known as the "Cape Geranium" or "Suid-Afrikaan Geranium," native to South Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with Pelargonium aestivale include relief from cold and flu symptoms, sore throats, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and reduce stress levels. Herbal preparations of Pelargonium aestivale are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
